criteria.description: deepgram_request_audit.py exists in the repo root and is import-safe: importing the module executes no network, no filesystem reads/writes, and no CLI parsing. All such side effects live under `if __name__ == "__main__":` or inside a main() function guarded that way.
criteria.verifyBy: A test does `import deepgram_request_audit` successfully with no network access and no ~/.voice-tutor-secrets.env present; the graded signal is this behavioral import-safety test passing under `python3 -m unittest discover -v` fully offline.

criteria.description: A pure function parses a Deepgram UTC `created` timestamp tolerantly on Python 3.9: it accepts a trailing 'Z' and returns a timezone-aware UTC datetime equal to the correct instant.
criteria.verifyBy: Unit test parses '2024-01-15T08:30:00Z' and asserts the result equals datetime(2024,1,15,8,30,0,tzinfo=timezone.utc).

criteria.description: The same tolerant parser handles the variants Deepgram may send — (a) fractional seconds with a trailing 'Z', (b) a plain UTC timestamp without any 'Z', and (c) fractional seconds without any 'Z' — returning the correct timezone-aware UTC datetime in each case. Because Deepgram's `created` field is documented UTC, an offset-less input is interpreted as UTC (not naive and not localized to any other zone). This proves it is not a bare fromisoformat, which on Python 3.9 rejects a trailing 'Z' and returns a naive datetime for a no-offset string.
criteria.verifyBy: Unit test asserts ALL THREE with exact pinned values: (a) parsing '2024-01-15T08:30:00.123456Z' yields datetime(2024,1,15,8,30,0,123456,tzinfo=timezone.utc); (b) parsing '2024-01-15T08:30:00' yields datetime(2024,1,15,8,30,0,tzinfo=timezone.utc) (offset-less interpreted as UTC); (c) parsing '2024-01-15T08:30:00.5' yields datetime(2024,1,15,8,30,0,500000,tzinfo=timezone.utc).

criteria.description: A UTC→local conversion function completes the bidirectional (↔) frame: given a timezone-aware UTC datetime it returns the equivalent America/Los_Angeles wall-clock time, correct across DST — a summer (PDT) instant maps back at UTC−7 and a winter (PST) instant at UTC−8.
criteria.verifyBy: Unit test asserts with pinned values: (summer) UTC datetime(2024,7,15,19,0,0,tzinfo=timezone.utc) → local wall-clock 2024-07-15 12:00:00 (hour==12, PDT); (winter) UTC datetime(2024,1,15,20,0,0,tzinfo=timezone.utc) → local wall-clock 2024-01-15 12:00:00 (hour==12, PST). Assertions compare the resulting America/Los_Angeles wall-clock components (year/month/day/hour/minute).

criteria.description: Local→UTC conversion treats a naive input as America/Los_Angeles local time and returns a timezone-aware UTC datetime, correct across DST: a Pacific Daylight Time timestamp converts by adding 7 hours (UTC−7) and a Pacific Standard Time timestamp by adding 8 hours (UTC−8).
criteria.verifyBy: Unit test asserts a summer date (naive local datetime(2024,7,15,12,0,0) → datetime(2024,7,15,19,0,0,tzinfo=timezone.utc), +7h) and a winter date (naive local datetime(2024,1,15,12,0,0) → datetime(2024,1,15,20,0,0,tzinfo=timezone.utc), +8h), each returned as a timezone-aware UTC datetime with the exact pinned instant.

criteria.description: test_deepgram_request_audit.py exists in the repo root and is discoverable and passing under `python3 -m unittest discover -v`, fully offline with stdlib only (no network, no secrets file, no third-party imports).
criteria.verifyBy: Run `python3 -m unittest discover -v`; all tests pass with no network access and no ~/.voice-tutor-secrets.env present.

scope.description: Only create deepgram_request_audit.py and test_deepgram_request_audit.py in the repo root; do not add unrelated modules, config, or packaging.

stdlib only — no requests, pytz, or other third-party packages; timezones MUST use stdlib zoneinfo.ZoneInfo('America/Los_Angeles') (not pytz and not a hardcoded fixed offset), HTTP (later sprints) via urllib.request.
Runtime must be read-only; parsing/conversion functions in this sprint must not write, create, or modify any file.
Never print, log, or echo credential values anywhere.

criteria.description: A pure ledger-parsing function (e.g. parse_ledger) accepts a file path OR an iterable of JSONL lines/strings and returns in-memory session structures, each carrying the session id, session_start and session_end parsed as naive America/Los_Angeles ISO-8601 timestamps, and session_duration_sec preserved as its numeric value. It performs no network access and no writes.
criteria.verifyBy: Unit test calls the function with an in-memory iterable of JSONL strings (fixtures) and asserts the returned structures contain the expected id; that session_start and session_end are naive datetime objects (tzinfo is None, not strings) equal to the expected local datetimes; and that session_duration_sec is preserved as a numeric value equal to the fixture value. Test does not touch the network or the secrets file.

criteria.description: A Deepgram-timestamp parser tolerantly parses the 'created' field on Python 3.9: it accepts a trailing 'Z', optional fractional seconds, and a value with no 'Z'/offset (treated as UTC since the field is documented UTC), and yields a timezone-aware UTC datetime, without relying on bare datetime.fromisoformat for the 'Z'/offset case.
criteria.verifyBy: Unit test parses fixture strings '2024-06-15T12:00:00Z', '2024-06-15T12:00:00.123456Z', and a no-Z '2024-06-15T12:00:00'; asserts each result is timezone-aware with UTC tzinfo and equals a constructed datetime(..., tzinfo=timezone.utc) at the expected instant (including the fractional microseconds for the fractional case).

criteria.description: Local↔UTC conversion places naive America/Los_Angeles ledger timestamps and UTC Deepgram timestamps into a single consistent frame using stdlib zoneinfo (ZoneInfo('America/Los_Angeles')), and is DST-correct: a PDT (summer) timestamp converts at −7h and a PST (winter) timestamp at −8h.
criteria.verifyBy: Unit test converts a concrete summer PDT naive local timestamp (e.g. 2024-07-15T12:00:00) and a concrete winter PST naive local timestamp (e.g. 2024-01-15T12:00:00) to UTC and asserts each equals a specific expected datetime(..., tzinfo=timezone.utc) (e.g. 2024-07-15T19:00:00Z and 2024-01-15T20:00:00Z respectively) — asserting equality to the exact UTC instant, not merely that the offset is 7h/8h, so a hard-coded month-based offset would be caught.

criteria.description: A matching function assigns each Deepgram request — derived from its parsed 'created' timestamp and billed duration in seconds — to ledger session(s) by time overlap in a single unified frame (Deepgram 'created' is UTC; ledger sessions are naive America/Los_Angeles; both are converted into one consistent frame before comparison). Overlap is defined as a positive-length intersection of the half-open intervals [session_start, session_end) and [created, created+duration); a mere touching endpoint (zero-length intersection) does NOT match. The function returns, per session, the set/list of matched requests and the Deepgram-billed seconds attributed to that session, plus a separate list of unmatched requests. Billed seconds are attributed whole (the request's full billed duration) to EACH session it overlaps. This whole-duration-per-session attribution is explicitly a PER-SESSION diagnostic view only: aggregate de-duplication of multiply-matched requests (so a straddling request is not double-counted in totals/overall ratio) is a deferred obligation of the reporting sprint and is NOT in scope for this sprint's matching function.
criteria.verifyBy: Unit tests over fixtures assert: (a) a request fully inside one session matches only that session (and no other), with the session's attributed billed seconds equal to the request's billed duration; (b) a request whose [created, created+duration) interval genuinely straddles the boundary between two adjacent (contiguous) sessions is matched to exactly those two sessions, WITH an explicit negative assertion that a third, non-overlapping session is NOT among the matches, AND the returned per-session attributed billed seconds are asserted for BOTH straddled sessions (whole billed duration attributed to each, per the documented per-session view); (c) a request overlapping no session appears in the unmatched list and matches no session; (d) a frame-unification test where the Deepgram request's 'created' is a UTC 'Z'-suffixed string (e.g. '2024-06-15T19:30:00Z') and the ledger session is a naive America/Los_Angeles window (e.g. 12:00–12:30 local on a PDT date) such that the match succeeds ONLY because the matcher converts both sides into one consistent frame — a naive same-string comparison would give the wrong answer; (e) a boundary fixture where a request's endpoint exactly touches a session boundary (created+duration == session_start, or created == session_end), asserting NO match per the half-open/positive-length rule. The function makes no network calls.

criteria.description: Ledger parsing and matching handle the empty-ledger case gracefully: parsing an empty iterable yields no sessions, and matching any requests against it yields all-unmatched (no error), with each unmatched request preserving its identity (its 'created' timestamp and billed duration).
criteria.verifyBy: Unit test parses an empty iterable and asserts zero sessions; matching sample requests against the empty ledger returns them all in the unmatched list, and the test asserts each unmatched entry retains its original 'created' timestamp and billed duration.

criteria.description: The main module (deepgram_request_audit.py) is import-safe: importing it performs no network, no file reads, and no writes; all CLI/network/file side effects are guarded under a main() invoked only via if __name__ == '__main__'.
criteria.verifyBy: Test file imports the module at module load and runs; unittest discovery (python3 -m unittest discover -v) passes fully offline with no secrets file present.

criteria.description: Tests reside in a file discoverable by 'python3 -m unittest discover -v' (e.g. test_deepgram_request_audit.py in repo root) and pass using stdlib only, offline, with no network and no reads of ~/.voice-tutor-secrets.env.
criteria.verifyBy: Run python3 -m unittest discover -v in the worktree; all tests pass with exit code 0 and no network/secrets access.

scope.description: Only net-new files deepgram_request_audit.py and its test file (e.g. test_deepgram_request_audit.py) should be created; do not add or modify unrelated modules, config, or packaging.
stdlib only — no requests/pytz/third-party packages; timezones via zoneinfo, HTTP (later sprints) via urllib.request.

Runtime code must remain read-only: no writing, creating, or modifying any file; this sprint's runtime logic (parsing/matching) touches no filesystem beyond reading the provided ledger path and performs no network I/O.
Do not print, log, or echo any credential values anywhere.

criteria.description: A single isolated fetch function (e.g. fetch_deepgram_requests) performs the paginated GET against https://api.deepgram.com/v1/projects/{project_id}/requests, accepting a page-fetcher seam (callback/injectable dependency) so tests can inject fixture page responses without any real HTTP; the function returns the assembled list of request records across all pages.
criteria.verifyBy: Unit test injects a fake page-fetcher returning multiple fixture pages and asserts the returned assembled list contains records from every page (not just the first).

criteria.description: Pagination termination is owned by the fetcher, not the test: the fetcher inspects each page's content (next-page URL / cursor / limit-offset signal) to decide whether to request another page, and stops only when a page signals no-more-data. It never stops at the first page when more pages are indicated, and does not stop early on a non-empty page that still signals more.
criteria.verifyBy: Unit test drives the fetcher with a seam whose returned pages carry the continuation signal in their content: a 3+ page sequence where the first pages are non-empty AND signal more, and the final page signals no-more; assert the seam is invoked once per page (proving the fetcher read the signal and continued) and all records across pages are collected. A separate single-page fixture (signals no-more) yields exactly one seam invocation. A test where an intermediate page is non-empty but still signals more proves the fetcher does not terminate early.

criteria.description: The real HTTP page fetch is implemented with urllib.request; the default page-fetcher builds a urllib.request.Request targeting the Deepgram requests endpoint (host api.deepgram.com, path .../projects/{project_id}/requests) and sets header Authorization to the literal 'Token ' concatenated with the actual API key value (f"Token {api_key}", not a placeholder). This default fetcher is what the isolated function uses when no seam is injected.
criteria.verifyBy: Unit test builds the default page-fetcher's urllib.request.Request object (without sending) and asserts req.get_header('Authorization') == 'Token ' + api_key for a sentinel api_key, and that the request full_url/host targets api.deepgram.com with a path containing the project id and '/requests'. No network call is made.

criteria.description: Date-range parameters are wired onto the outgoing request: when start/end dates (YYYY-MM-DD, from --start/--end) are provided, the default page-fetcher's urllib.request.Request URL carries them as the appropriate query parameters (e.g. start=/end=). When omitted, the fetcher requests the full available range (no start/end constraint on the query). This ensures the fetch window is not silently ignored.
criteria.verifyBy: Unit test builds the default request Request object (no network) with sentinel start='2024-03-01' and end='2024-03-31' and asserts both sentinel dates appear in the request full_url/query string under start/end parameters. A second test builds the request with start/end omitted and asserts no start=/end= query parameter is emitted (full-range behavior).

criteria.description: Response normalization converts a raw Deepgram page/response (using a realistic Deepgram envelope, e.g. a 'requests' array whose records carry the billed duration under the response's actual duration field name rather than a pre-named 'seconds' key) into a consistent internal shape exposing at least each request's 'created' UTC timestamp and its billed duration expressed in seconds, deriving/extracting the seconds value from that envelope field.
criteria.verifyBy: Unit test feeds a fixture Deepgram response dict using the realistic envelope field for duration (not a pre-named 'seconds' passthrough) and asserts each normalized record exposes the expected created timestamp value and the exact numeric billed-duration-in-seconds value correctly extracted from that field, proving actual normalization rather than an identity map.

criteria.description: A credential-loading function directly parses the KEY=VALUE env file (default path ~/.voice-tutor-secrets.env) for DEEPGRAM_API_KEY and DEEPGRAM_PROJECT_ID, accepting file content/path via argument so it does not require the variables to be exported in the process environment; it handles blank lines, ignores comments, and tolerates surrounding whitespace.
criteria.verifyBy: Unit test passes a fixture env string (with blank/comment lines and surrounding whitespace) to the parser and asserts DEEPGRAM_API_KEY equals its sentinel value and DEEPGRAM_PROJECT_ID equals its sentinel value exactly, with no reliance on os.environ.

criteria.description: No credential value leaks through the concrete non-header, non-path surfaces of the fetch/credential functions. Specifically: (a) the request full_url / query string must not contain the API key at all, and must not embed the project id as a secret beyond its legitimate URL-path placement (i.e. the project id appearing in the endpoint path is allowed; it must not appear in a query string); (b) any HTTP error/exception message and any stdout/user-facing output produced by the default fetcher on failure must never contain the API key, and must not contain the project id except where it is echoed as part of the mandated request URL path. The Authorization header carrying 'Token <key>' (per c3) is a mandated destination and is explicitly excluded from this leak check.
criteria.verifyBy: Unit test uses sentinel secret values and asserts: (a) the default request's full_url/query string does not contain the sentinel API key, and the project id does not appear in the query string (its presence in the URL path is allowed); and (b) at least one HTTP error/exception path (e.g. the default fetcher raising/handling a failure) produces an exception message and/or stdout that contains the sentinel API key nowhere, and contains the sentinel project id only (if at all) as part of the request URL path. The Authorization header value is not asserted against.

criteria.description: The credential loader is strictly read-only: given injected file content or a path it parses and returns values without creating, writing, or modifying any file.
criteria.verifyBy: Unit test invokes the credential loader with injected fixture content/path and asserts the returned values are correct; test confirms (e.g. via a temp dir snapshot or by passing content directly) that no file is written or created by the call.

criteria.description: All fetch/credential/file-reading side effects live behind functions invoked only from a main() guarded by if __name__ == "__main__"; importing the module performs no network, file, or CLI side effects.
criteria.verifyBy: Test imports the module while sentinel patches on the network/file/CLI entry points are already installed (fresh import via subprocess or importlib.reload under the patches) and asserts none of those side-effecting entry points were invoked by the import, so the assertion is meaningful rather than vacuously true.

scope.description: Only net-new files deepgram_request_audit.py and its test file (e.g. test_deepgram_request_audit.py) in the repo root are added/modified; no other modules, config, or packaging.
stdlib only — urllib.request for HTTP, no requests/pytz/third-party packages.
Runtime is strictly read-only for network/credential concerns in this sprint: the fetcher and credential loader must not write, create, or modify any file (no cache/temp/output files).

This sprint focuses on the isolated fetcher, credential loading, and pagination/normalization seam; matching/timezone/reporting logic may be stubbed or deferred and is not required here beyond what normalization exposes.

criteria.description: A pure function computes, per session, a structured summary keyed by session id/date containing: (a) wall-clock seconds derived by a SINGLE deterministic, documented rule that is applied IDENTICALLY everywhere it is needed (per-session, aggregate, text, and JSON) — the generator selects and documents the rule (e.g. session_duration_sec, or session_end minus session_start); (b) summed Deepgram-billed seconds from that session's matched requests; (c) delta = wall-clock minus billed; (d) ratio = billed / wall-clock, and when wall-clock is zero the ratio is set to a fixed sentinel value None (not raised, not 0).
criteria.verifyBy: Offline unit test feeds fixture matched data (sessions with matched request durations) and asserts summed billed seconds, delta, and ratio per session; asserts the wall-clock value used in the per-session summary is IDENTICAL to the value the same module uses when building the aggregate/text/JSON for that session (internal consistency of whatever documented rule is chosen — not a mandated tie-break winner); a zero-wall-clock session asserts ratio is exactly None (the documented sentinel) with no exception.

criteria.description: A pure function computes aggregate totals across all sessions (total wall-clock sec, total billed sec, total delta) and overall ratio (total billed / total wall-clock), in consistent seconds units. A Deepgram request that straddles two adjacent sessions (and is therefore attributed to each per-session summary by the Sprint-2 matcher) is counted EXACTLY ONCE in the aggregate total billed seconds, de-duplicated by a stable per-request identity taken from the request's raw.request_id when present, else the (created, billed_sec) pair; this SAME identity rule is applied identically here (in-memory aggregate) and in c5's JSON aggregate. The overall ratio reflects this de-duplicated billed total.
criteria.verifyBy: Offline unit test builds fixture matched data with at least one request straddling two sessions (appearing in both sessions' per-session billed sums) and asserts: (1) the aggregate total billed seconds counts that request's duration once, not twice — aggregate billed is strictly less than the naive sum of per-session billed sums by exactly the straddler's duration; (2) total wall-clock is hand-computed using the SAME documented wall-clock rule chosen in c1 (not an independently assumed rule), and total delta and overall ratio equal hand-computed values based on that wall-clock total and the de-duplicated billed total; (3) an over-dedup guard: two genuinely distinct matched requests are NOT collapsed by the identity rule — e.g. two distinct requests (distinct raw.request_id) matched to the same session, even with identical billed_sec, both count in that session's billed sum and in the aggregate.

criteria.description: A deterministic text-table renderer produces the per-session table (columns: session id/date, wall-clock seconds, summed billed seconds, delta, ratio) from a summary structure with no network/file access; identical input yields byte-identical output.
criteria.verifyBy: Offline unit test calls the renderer on a fixed summary and asserts that each session's rendered row contains that session's actual computed values (its id/date, wall-clock sec, billed sec, delta, and ratio as they appear in the summary), not merely a self-authored full-string blob; a second call on the same input asserts byte-identical output.

criteria.description: The report renders a separate unmatched-requests listing showing each unmatched request's timestamp and billed duration in seconds.
criteria.verifyBy: Offline unit test provides fixture unmatched requests and asserts the rendered listing contains each request's timestamp and duration; the empty case renders a clear 'none'/empty listing without error.

criteria.description: A --json machine-readable mode emits a JSON-serializable structure carrying the same information as the text report: per-session summaries (id/date, wall-clock sec, billed sec, delta, ratio), the unmatched-requests list (timestamps + durations), and aggregate totals + overall ratio — where the aggregate billed total is the same de-duplicated value used by the text report, de-duplicated by the SAME stable per-request identity rule as c2 (raw.request_id when present, else (created, billed_sec)), so a straddling request is counted once identically across the in-memory and JSON aggregates.
criteria.verifyBy: Offline unit test builds the report object from fixture data (including a straddling request), asserts json.loads(json.dumps(obj)) round-trips and contains the expected per-session, unmatched, and aggregate keys/values matching the text report's data, and asserts the JSON aggregate billed total equals the de-duplicated total (straddler counted once) and is exactly identical to c2's in-memory aggregate billed total for the same fixture.

criteria.description: Report output (both text and JSON) states, for EACH side, the actual time window (start and end) AND a timezone label: the local ledger side shows its window with an America/Los_Angeles label and the Deepgram side shows its window with a UTC label, with seconds as the consistent unit on both sides. When a side has no rows, its window is rendered as an explicit empty/placeholder value (still carrying the side's timezone label) without error.
criteria.verifyBy: Offline unit test asserts that BOTH the rendered text output AND the JSON structure include the ledger side's start and end window values with an America/Los_Angeles timezone label, and the Deepgram side's start and end window values with a UTC label; a second case with an empty ledger and/or zero requests asserts that in BOTH the text output AND the JSON structure each empty side renders an explicit empty/placeholder window while still carrying its timezone label, without raising.

criteria.description: The CLI is wired under a guarded main(): flags --ledger (required path), optional --start/--end as YYYY-MM-DD, and --json. When --start/--end are omitted, the fetch uses the full available range (no date filter passed). Importing the module executes no CLI parsing, network, or file I/O.
criteria.verifyBy: Offline unit test imports the module (asserting no side effects), and inspects the argparse parser via a build_parser() factory function that returns the argparse.ArgumentParser without side effects, confirming --ledger/--start/--end/--json are accepted and that parsing fixture argv omitting --start/--end yields None/full-range semantics; verifier `python3 -m unittest discover -v` runs offline.

criteria.description: The reporting/computation functions are pure (return strings/JSON-serializable objects; the CLI emits output only via print to stdout) and never leak credential values: rendering data whose request/raw rows contain credential-looking values must not emit any DEEPGRAM_API_KEY or DEEPGRAM_PROJECT_ID value into either the text or JSON output.
criteria.verifyBy: Offline unit test renders fixture data whose matched/raw request rows embed sentinel credential-looking strings and asserts those sentinel DEEPGRAM_API_KEY / DEEPGRAM_PROJECT_ID values appear nowhere in the text-table output nor in the json.dumps output; all reporting/computation is exercised with stdlib only, no network, no API keys, no secrets file, under `python3 -m unittest discover -v`.

scope.description: Only deepgram_request_audit.py and its test file may be modified/created; no new modules, config, or packaging.

Reuse existing fetch/matching/timezone functions from prior sprints; do not reimplement the network fetcher or matching logic. The aggregate de-duplication is new reporting logic that post-processes matcher output using request identity, not a rewrite of matching.
stdlib only (argparse, json, datetime, zoneinfo, urllib) — no requests/pytz/third-party packages; the network fetcher stays isolated so all reporting logic is testable offline.
Runtime strictly read-only: output goes only to stdout; no cache/output/temp files; never print or log credential values.
